description:
The castle was purposefully build on an small rocky island in the middle of a deep and very rapidly flowing straight between 2 lake systems, which was frequently used as a waterway for trading and war during history. This also meant that the castle's "moat" (the straight itself) would not freeze over during even the harshest of winters. There was a well for sweetwater on the island, though it had to be dug unseemly deep and was even then prone to drying up at times.

The castle had originally 5 nearly equally tall round towers built to similar design like those on your picture (cannon towers), of which 1 was partially dismantled due to perceived danger of imminent collapse and the other one in fact did collapse due to neglect, they were located on the castle's "backside" where the water was wider (upper lake) and so were not rebuilt but were replaced with bastion defences later on.

The original castle was triangular (not square) in shape and had 3 towers in each of it corners 2 remain and 1 was destroyed.

Later on the castle was expanded first by adding 2 more towers and curtain walls to cover the islands only water source (the well) now covered by the huge white canvas. This also allowed more substantial storage space and accommodations to be placed secure on the island. One of the additional round towers is still seen on the upper-right corner of the castle here, the other (named the "fat tower" due its originally 5 meter thick walls, since it was intended to be used, as a second "keep" at the time) is now covered by the huge bastion seen in the lower-right corner of the castle, the defences facing the upper lake were later on strengthened with further 2 large bastions and 2 additional small ones added in the upper left-corner and the middle of the right flank of the castle closely adjoining the original "Fat Tower" or the "Grand Bastion" as it became known. The original triangular Medieval Castle served as the "Keep" for the whole complex, with its walls maintained as clearly the highest in all the castle. 

"The Castle Front" side facing the town of Savonlinna (meaning "the castle of Savolax", the town was named after the province this castle and its town administered and protected). 
[Text: Ilari Rahja]
wikipedia_extract:
Olavinlinna, also known as St. Olaf's Castle, is a 15th-century three-tower castle located in Savonlinna, Finland. It is built on an island in the Kyrönsalmi strait that connects the lakes Haukivesi and Pihlajavesi. It is the northernmost medieval stone fortress still standing and also the best preserved of all the medieval castles in the Swedish realm.
The castle forms a spectacular stage for the Savonlinna Opera Festival, which was held for the first time in the summer of 1912.
heritage_status: building protected by Decree 480/85 in Finland

wikipedia_extract: Turku Castle is a medieval structure in the city of Turku, Finland. Together with Turku Cathedral, the castle is one of the oldest buildings still in use in Finland. It is also the largest surviving medieval building in Finland. It was founded in the late 13th century and stands on the banks of the Aura River. The castle served as a bastion and administrative center in the historical region of Finland until the early 19th century. It played a role in power struggles within Sweden and the Kalmar Union and stood sieges, with additional battles fought outside its walls. The castle was at its peak in the mid-16th century, during the reign of Duke John of Finland and Catherine Jagellon. It lost its status as an administrative center in the 17th century, after Per Brahe's period as governor-general of Finland came to an end. Turku Castle is Finland's most visited museum. Visitations reach well over 100,000 people annually. Some of the rooms in the castle are used for municipal functions.
heritage_status: Cultural Heritage Site of National Significance

wikipedia_extract: Häme Castle or Tavastia Castle is a medieval castle in Kanta-Häme, Finland. It is located in Hämeenlinna, the city between Helsinki and Tampere.
heritage_status: Cultural Heritage Site of National Significance

wikipedia_extract: Raseborg is a town in Finland, located in the southern coast of the country. Raseborg is situated in the western part of the Uusimaa region. The population of Raseborg is approximately 27,000, while the sub-region has a population of approximately 40,000. It is the 39th most populous municipality in Finland.

Kastelholm Castle is a medieval castle built by the Swedes in the late 14th century. It is located near Road 2 in the municipality of Sund in Åland, Finland, about 25 kilometres (16 mi) northeast of Mariehamn, overlooking a fjord near the village of Kastelholm.

Kuusisto Castle was a medieval episcopal castle on the island of Kuusisto in Kaarina, Finland, near Turku. The castle was probably built in the early 14th century, although the site seems to have been a bishop's residence by the 1290s.
